page: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCSv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon E-2288G processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and 102 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, as evidenced by a gzip compression rate of 34.48 GB/sec. Single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall within the average range. However, due to the low core count, multi-core benchmarks are weak, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the bottom 25% of tested hardware.

From a resource density perspective, the DC2s_v2 offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, aligning with standard general-purpose profiles. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution times for single-threaded tasks, though the lack of hyperthreading limits parallel processing efficiency. This server is best suited for lightweight workloads, basic web serving, single-threaded data processing, and development environments that do not require heavy multi-threaded compute.
description: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DCSv2 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E-2288G processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs at a 3.7 GHz clock speed, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and 102 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage. Benchmark data highlights strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression reaching 34.48 GB/sec, while multi-core performance remains weak due to the dual-core limitation. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that rely on high single-core clock speeds rather than massive parallelization. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, and basic development environments.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core server featuring strong single-threaded performance and balanced memory allocation for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon E-2288G processor operating at 3.7 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
102 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-threaded performance with 34.48 GB/sec gzip compression
Weak multi-core performance due to a 2-core, non-hyperthreaded design
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded processing
